---
layout: post
title: "Sophia Folsom Pitchlynn"
date: 2021-12-12 18:22:00 +0000
published: false
---

Sophia Folsom 1773-1881 married Major John Pitchlynn 1764-1834 in 1804.&nbsp; Sophia's son, Peter, was Chief of the Choctaw tribe from 1864-1866 and was instrumental in Indian negotiations with the U.S. Government.

While looking into hints on Ancestry.com, I ran across a slave schedule dated 13 Aug 1860.&nbsp; That schedule indicated Sophia was a slave owner.&nbsp; I found this interesting as I didn't have it in my head that Native Americans were slave owners. OK, I guess it was in there somewhere, but not at the forefront.&nbsp; I always saw Native Americans as adopting black people into the tribes, not owning them.&nbsp; However, she was married to Major John Pitchlynn.&nbsp; According to <a href="https://www.405magazine.com/an-oklahoma-story-in-stone/" target="_blank">405 Magazine</a>, John Pitchlynn had accumulated "...50 slaves..".&nbsp; John passed away in 1834, 26 years before the slave schedule showing Sophia to own slaves.

<img alt="" height="320" src="data:image/png;base64," width="320" />
